name: CRISPR Editing Efficiency Predictor
description: Predicts on-target editing efficiency BEFORE the experiment is run. Uses a simplified linear approximation of Doench 2016 Rule Set 2: position-specific nucleotide weights along the protospacer, PAM context (NGG > NAG, NGGT > NGGA), GC content optimum, and poly-T penalty (Pol III termination). Then adjusts for delivery method (RNP, plasmid, lentivirus, AAV, electroporation) and editing outcome (NHEJ knockout, HDR knockin, base/prime editing). Returns a predicted % efficiency with a +/-15% confidence range, a feature breakdown, and human-readable warnings.

inputs.name: protospacer
inputs.description: 20bp DNA (Cas9) or 23bp DNA (Cas12a) protospacer, no PAM.

inputs.name: pam
inputs.description: PAM sequence adjacent to the protospacer. 3bp NGG for Cas9 (3' of protospacer), 4bp TTTV for Cas12a (5' of protospacer).

inputs.name: downstream_3nt
inputs.description: Cas9 only. 3 nucleotides immediately 3' of the PAM. Used for NGGT vs NGGA preference.

outputs.description: Dict with on_target_efficiency_pct, confidence_range, feature_contributions breakdown, warnings, and a plain-English interpretation.
